items.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Pre-Auth API Bypass (CVSS 9.1) Actively Exploited
items.summary: Fortinet patched a critical pre-authentication API access bypass in FortiClient EMS (CVSS 9.1) enabling privilege escalation. CISA added it to its KEV catalog on April 6, 2026 with a 3-day federal patch deadline.
items.why: Pre-auth privilege escalation in a widely deployed enterprise VPN client with confirmed in-the-wild exploitation and a 3-day federal patch deadline signals high attacker interest and broad organizational risk.

items.title: CISA Adds Linux Kernel LPE Bug CVE-2026-31431 ('Copy Fail') to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
items.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-31431, a Linux kernel local privilege escalation flaw (CVSS 7.8), to its KEV catalog after confirmed active exploitation. The bug lets an unprivileged local user gain root access.
items.why: Linux kernel LPE bugs reaching CISA's KEV are being leveraged in post-exploitation chains; defenders should prioritize kernel patching across all Linux infrastructure immediately.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours, Pivots to Cloud C2 via filen.io
items.summary: Russia-linked APT28 (Fancy Bear) targeted European military and government entities by weaponizing a newly disclosed Microsoft Office 1-day within 24 hours of disclosure. The group abuses legitimate cloud storage filen.io as C2 to evade network detection.
items.why: Sub-24-hour weaponization cycles and cloud-service C2 abuse demonstrate nation-state operational speed; defenders need near-real-time patching and cloud egress monitoring to counter this TTPs.

items.title: Unit 42: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploys 6 New RAT Variants (MiniUpdate, MiniJunk V2) Across 5 Countries
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 tracked Iranian APT Screening Serpens deploying six new RAT variants across five countries since February 2026, split into two families: MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2, purpose-built for persistent espionage following regional conflict escalation.
items.why: Rapid development of multiple distinct RAT families tied to a geopolitical trigger shows a maturing threat actor scaling operations quickly; government and critical infrastructure targets in the affected regions face elevated risk.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: New PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL Malware Families Query LLMs at Runtime to Evade Detection
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report documents PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL — malware families that actively call large language models mid-execution to generate evasive payloads and bypass behavioral detection. Attackers are also now handing off access in as little as 22 seconds post-compromise.
items.why: LLM-querying malware is a paradigm shift: signatures and static behavior models cannot predict dynamically generated payloads. Defenders must add runtime LLM-call monitoring and anomaly detection to their stacks.
